title: Investigate, identify, and fix admin deposit page missing records and wrong status display
issue_description: Investigate an issue where the admin deposit page may not be showing all deposits and may not correctly display whether deposits are pending or completed. Systems involved may include AlgonneyPay, treasury events, algonney.com backend, database queries, admin API endpoints, and frontend table/status mapping. Work step by step with one and only one step per cycle. First investigate the full deposit data path end-to-end, then identify and prove the exact root cause, then propose and implement the fix, and finally validate that the page shows all deposits correctly and displays the correct pending/completed status. Changes are allowed only after the issue is clearly proven. Prefer read-only investigation first, then move to fix mode only when the root cause is confirmed. Stop only when the issue is fully resolved, blocked, or requires explicit human approval for a risky action.
issue_seed_prompt: Investigate, identify, and fix admin deposit page missing records and wrong status display

history.step_title: Inspect admin deposits API contract and real response shape
history.prompt_for_claude:
Investigate ONLY the admin deposits backend/API contract in read-only mode.

Goal:
Find the exact admin deposits endpoint used by algonney.com admin, then inspect its handler/service/query and determine:

the exact response JSON shape/key that contains the deposit rows

the exact status field/value(s) returned for each deposit

whether the backend is already returning all rows correctly or filtering some out

Do not change code. Do not inspect frontend yet except only enough to identify which endpoint it calls.

What to do:

Find the admin deposit page request target (endpoint URL/path).

Trace that endpoint in backend code to its handler/service/database query.

Show the response contract and status vocabulary with concrete file paths and line references.

If possible, capture a sample local response shape from existing code/tests/logs/mock data; if not possible, state the inferred shape from code only.

Respond with:

endpoint path

handler/service/query chain

response JSON keys for rows

returned status values / mapping

whether backend appears to include all deposits or already drops some

the single strongest finding from this step only
history.claude_output:
SUMMARY:
The backend endpoint GET /api/v1/admin/deposits returns rows under the key items with raw DB field status containing values pending, confirmed, credited, rejected, below_minimum, or manual_review. The backend returns all matching deposits correctly — no rows are dropped by the
backend — but the frontend reads the wrong response key (deposits instead of items), causing the empty table.

FILES:
- path: /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/routes/admin_deposit_routes.py
- function: list_deposits_for_review (line 80)
- path: /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/models/deposit_models.py
- function: DepositTransactionStatus enum

FLOW:
1. Frontend calls GET /api/v1/admin/deposits?page=1&page_size=20&sort=submitted_at&order=desc (optionally &status=<value>)
2. Handler list_deposits_for_review (admin_deposit_routes.py:80) receives status, limit (default 50), cursor — ignores page, page_size, sort, order
3. Builds MongoDB query: {} (all), or { requires_manual_review: True } if status=="pending_review", or { status: <value> } for any other status string
4. Queries deposit_transactions collection: .find(query).sort("_id", -1).limit(limit+1) — no joins, no projection, returns all fields from raw document
5. Renames _id → id (str), builds list
6. Returns AdminDepositListResponse: { "items": [...], "total": <int>, "has_more": <bool> } (admin_deposit_routes.py:31–35, 124–128)

FINDINGS:
- Response key is items (admin_deposit_routes.py:31): class AdminDepositListResponse(BaseModel): items: List[Dict]. The deposit rows array is returned under key "items".
- Backend returns all matching deposits — no WHERE clause drops rows unless status filter is explicitly passed. Default (no filter) returns every document in deposit_transactions.
- Actual status values stored/returned come from DepositTransactionStatus enum (deposit_models.py:21–28): "pending", "confirmed", "credited", "rejected", "below_minimum", "manual_review". These are raw strings passed through unchanged in the response.
- On admin approve (line 317): "status": "credited" is written — not "approved". Value "approved" is never written to deposit_transactions.
- On admin reject (line 389): "status": "rejected" is written.
- Backend does not join user data in the list endpoint — raw documents contain user_id (string) and coin (not currency), with timestamp field created_at (not submitted_at).
- Inferred response shape for one row: { "id": "<str>", "user_id": "<str>", "coin": "USDT", "amount": <float>, "network": "BEP20", "tx_hash": "<str>", "status": "credited"|"pending"|..., "created_at": "<datetime>", "requires_manual_review": <bool>, ... }

LIKELY_BREAKPOINTS:
- Strongest finding: Frontend reads data?.deposits (DepositsSection.tsx:243) but backend sends data.items — this single mismatch makes the table permanently empty regardless of DB content.
- Status value "approved" sent by frontend filter does not exist in DB — filter returns 0 rows; "credited" is the correct post-approval value.

RECOMMENDED_NEXT_CHECK:
- Fix data?.deposits → data?.items in DepositsSection.tsx:243 and verify AdminDepositsResponse type matches { items: AdminDeposit[]; total: number; has_more: boolean } — this is the single change that will restore all missing records.
- Then update DepositStatus type and statusVariantMap to include credited, confirmed, manual_review, below_minimum to fix wrong/missing status badges.
<<>>
history.claude_summary: The backend endpoint GET /api/v1/admin/deposits returns rows under the key items with raw DB field status containing values pending, confirmed, credited, rejected, below_minimum, or manual_review. The backend returns all matching deposits correctly — no rows are dropped by the backend — but the frontend reads the wrong response key (deposits instead of items), causing the empty table.
history.planner_interpretation: The first highest-priority read-only step is to verify what the admin deposits backend endpoint actually returns and how deposit status is represented. This establishes the source-of-truth contract before checking frontend mapping, and it can immediately prove whether missing rows or wrong statuses begin at the API layer or later in the UI.

stall_reason: Unknown error type: BrowserBridgeError: Failed to get response: ChatGPT response timed out after 120000ms
